stdout:
**Acceptance Criteria**

No blocking findings.

- REQ-01: Arbitrary markdown project sources are supported via config-declared `source` and covered by `test_config_declared_markdown_kind_indexes_project_items` in [markdown_kind_source_path_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/markdown_kind_source_path_tests.rs:139).
- REQ-02: Registry-first kinds now use a generic TOML descriptor (`toml`, `table`, `id-field`, `text-field`, `link-fields`) in [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/config.rs:557) and project through `load_toml_scope_items` in [item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:1086). Non-invariant coverage exists in [item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:751).
- REQ-03: `ItemKind::from_config` branches on scope/source-of-truth, not kind name, in [item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:452). The `invariant` ordinary-config regression is covered in command/domain tests.
- REQ-04: `jit init` emits editable `[item_kinds]` defaults from the hierarchy template in [hierarchy_templates.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/hierarchy_templates.rs:181), pinned by [init_item_kinds_golden.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/init_item_kinds_golden.rs:90). `resolve_item_kinds(None)` returns empty, so there is no hidden built-in fallback in [item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:1219).
- REQ-05: Drift only reports declared-but-unenforced; reverse enforced-but-undeclared drift is removed in [drift.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/drift.rs:12).
- REQ-06: Region projection reads and writes the configured target through storage in [projection.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/projection.rs:305), with CLAUDE.md region coverage in [projection.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/projection.rs:601).
- REQ-07 is aspirational and not required.

**Architecture**

The separation of concerns is preserved. Generic parsing/projection is pure domain code in [item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:1086). Commands orchestrate config, storage, and domain logic in [commands/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:194). Repository-local reads/writes go through `IssueStore::read_repo_file` / `write_repo_file` in [storage/mod.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/storage/mod.rs:240).

**Functional Paradigm**

The new core paths mostly use iterator pipelines (`load_toml_scope_items`, link-field projection, drift computation) and keep mutation to orchestration/storage boundaries. The few loops in command code are pragmatic I/O orchestration.

**Correctness And Invariants**

Atomic writes are preserved for new write paths: `jit init` uses `write_file_atomic` in [main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:925), and projection writes through `write_repo_file`, which uses the shared atomic writer in [json.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:825). State-change/event, gate-transition, assignee/label grammar, and PID-safety invariants are not regressed by these changes. PID use found here records metadata or temp-file names only; no `kill(-1, sig)` risk is introduced.

**Safety**

`#![deny(unsafe_code)]` is present in both [lib.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/lib.rs:6) and [main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:14). No unsafe code was introduced.

**Testing**

I did not run cargo tests because the issue has a passed cargo-ci gate. Evidence: `.jit/gate-runs/5b9f6539-ad61-4f99-853e-3596148ec1cd/result.json` reports `fmt: ok`, `clippy: ok`, and `test: 2746 passed, 0 failed, 12 ignored` at commit `8304d42939e186f4409817c14ee40bd752ea5258`.

I did run `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it passed with `Repository validation passed`.

**Error Handling**

New fallible paths return typed or contextual errors: TOML projection maps parse/type/missing-field failures to `ItemError` in [item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:1091), and command wrappers add source/kind context in [commands/item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:214). I did not find new `unwrap()`/`expect()` use in non-test library paths related to this change.

**CLI Contract**

The new/affected surfaces support `--json`: item commands extract JSON mode in [main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:531), and invariant `render`/`check` expose JSON flags in [cli.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/cli.rs:400).

**Documentation**

Public new config/source types and functions have doc comments with examples, including `ItemKindSource` in [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/config.rs:444), `TomlSourceDescriptor` in [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/config.rs:557), and `project_invariants` in [projection.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/projection.rs:265). Minor non-blocking note: one projection doc link names an old path for `write_file_atomic`, but cargo-ci did not fail on docs and behavior is correct.

**Jit Issue Management**

All listed dependencies in the issue context are `done`. There is no prior review history to re-check. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passes; label warnings are not present in the run output.

VERDICT: PASS
---
Gate result: PASSED
